content: Speckle's Grasshopper/Dynamo connectors delegate account/token management to a separate desktop app (Speckle Manager) or an Account Select node — the canvas node does not hold the raw secret in the normal flow. For headless/automation cases, a discrete 'Account from Server Token' / developer-access-token node accepts a pasted token string with specific required scopes (profile:read, stream:read, workspace:read, etc.); tokens are minted in the Speckle web app UI, not in the canvas. This node-vs-managed-account split (managed account for interactive use, explicit token node for automation) is the dominant AEC-canvas pattern.
